## v2.0.2

### Fixed
- **"Open With…" showed a blank/incomplete application list, and double-clicking a text file opened the "Open With" dialog instead of the file, when the container runs as root (`PUID=0`)** (#22). Two root causes, both fixed:
  - Under `PUID=0` LinuxServer cannot remap `abc` onto uid 0, so Krusader runs as **root** and read its KDE config and the KSycoca service cache from root's own `$HOME`, not the `/config` tree the container populated as `abc`. The desktop session now pins `XDG_CONFIG_HOME`, `XDG_DATA_HOME` and `XDG_CACHE_HOME` to the persistent `/config` locations for **every** PUID (and rebuilds the cache in-session), and ensures `/usr/share/applications` stays on the scan path — so root and the default non-root user behave identically. The stale KF5 `ksycoca5_*` leftover is purged.
  - The image shipped **no default text-file association**, so a double-click had no registered handler and fell back to the "Open With" dialog, where Kate was not offered. A `mimeapps.list` now registers **Kate** as the default handler for `text/plain` and common text types, so a double-click opens Kate directly and Kate appears in "Open With".
  - **The application index itself was empty**: `kbuildsycoca6` indexes applications through the XDG menu spec, but the image had no `applications.menu` (plasma-workspace only ships `plasma-applications.menu`, which is only picked up inside a full Plasma session). It logged `"applications.menu" not found` and built a KSycoca without any application services — so even a correct `mimeapps.list` could not resolve Kate. The image now ships a minimal include-everything `/etc/xdg/menus/applications.menu`, and both cache rebuilds run with `--noincremental` so deployed associations can never be skipped by a stale-timestamp shortcut.
- **The devices button in the status bar showed a different (blue) folder icon than the file list, and icons ignored the colours configured in Konfigurator → Colors.** The button now uses the **exact same folder icon the file panel shows for a directory** (`inode-directory` → `folder`), matched size-for-size, and on every start it is tinted to the **configured status-bar foreground colour** (the colour the bar's text already uses). The **file list's folder icons follow the configured panel foreground colour the same way** — set a custom panel foreground and the folder icons take that colour; with no custom colour they keep the stock theme tint. Colour changes apply on the next container start. A stale KDE icon cache (`icon-cache.kcache`) that survives image updates on the persistent `/config` volume — and kept serving the old icon — is now purged on start, so icon changes actually take effect.
- **The UI language (`KRUSADER_LANG`) was silently ignored — the UI stayed English although the language was configured correctly.** The desktop session on the Selkies base does not inherit the s6 container environment, so Krusader launched with the image's English locale defaults (`LANG=C.UTF-8`) no matter what the language hook had set. The session autostart now sources the language snippet directly before starting Krusader, so the configured language reliably reaches the app. The Unraid template's language dropdown also shows written-out names now (`de = Deutsch`, `en = English`, …) — the container extracts the leading code.
- **KDE platform integration was not active inside the session.** The desktop session kept the image's `QT_QPA_PLATFORMTHEME=qt5ct` (the s6 environment does not reach the session), so Qt never loaded the KDE platform plugin and parts of `kdeglobals` — including the icon-theme switch — were ignored. The session now forces the KDE platform theme unconditionally. The desktop also waits for the container init to finish (s6 dependency), closing a first-boot race, and several hardenings landed: colour parsing clamps invalid values, the "Use KDE default colors" checkbox correctly disables the icon tint, and the whole session-start chain is POSIX-sh-safe (openbox runs it with `sh`, not bash — a bashism silently disabled the language block).
- **Icons never switched with the theme.** The shipped `kdeglobals` hardcoded `[Icons] Theme=breeze-dark`, so under `KRUSADER_THEME=light` every icon (file list and status bar alike) kept its dark-theme tint. The icon theme now follows the selected theme (`breeze` for light, `breeze-dark` for dark), so all icons flip together on a theme change.
